heading: Compensation for loss or destruction of commercial fishing vessel or gear
label: 1980 Compensation for loss or destruction of commercial fishing vessel or gear

L. 95–376Subsec. Compensation for Certain Fishing Vessel and Gear Damage; Application, (b)(1), , , 3301, provided that:Pub. L. 96–561, title II, § 240(a)94 Stat. 3300Dec. 22, 1980“(a).—In GeneralIf—“(1)any owner or operator of a fishing vessel who suffered, after , and before the date of the enactment of this title [], damage to, or loss or destruction of, such vessel or fishing gear used with such vessel, but did not apply for compensation therefor under section 10 of the Fishermen’s Protective Act of 1967 () within the 60-day period prescribed in subsection (c)(1) of such section; orSeptember 17, 1978Dec. 22, 198022 U.S.C. 1980“(2)any commercial fisherman who suffered, after , and before the date of the enactment of this title, damages compensable under title IV of the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act of 1978 ( et seq.), but who did not timely file a claim therefor within the 60-day period prescribed in section 405(a) of such Act [];September 17, 197843 U.S.C. 184143 U.S.C. 1845(a)such owner or operator may make application for compensation with respect to such damage, loss or destruction under such section 10 [this section], and such commercial fisherman may file a claim for, compensation for such damages under such title IV [ et seq.], to the Secretary of Commerce, within the 60-day period beginning on the date of the enactment of this title [].43 U.S.C. 1841Dec. 22, 1980“(b).—Special Provisions(1)Notwithstanding any other provision of law—“(A)any application or filing timely made under subsection (a) shall be treated by the Secretary of Commerce as an application timely made under such section 10(c)(1) [subsec. (c)(1) of this section], or as a filing timely made under such section 405(a) [], as the case may be, with respect to the damage, loss, or destruction claimed; and43 U.S.C. 1845(a)“(B)any claim for fishing gear loss that was pending on , before the United States-Union of Soviet Socialist Republics Fisheries Claims Board or the American-Spanish Fisheries Board shall be treated by the Secretary of Commerce as a timely application made, on the date of the enactment of this title [], under such section 10(c)(1) [subsec. (c)(1) of this section] for compensation for such loss.”June 1, 1980Dec. 22, 1980

Timely Applications for Compensation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 96–289, § 4(a)94 Stat. 605June 28, 1980“Notwithstanding the provisions of section 10(c)(1) of the Fishermens Protective Act of 1967 () [subsec. (c)(1) of this section] applications for compensation under such Act [this chapter], filed within 90 days after the date of enactment of this subsection [] shall be deemed to be timely filed.”22 U.S.C. 1980June 28, 1980

children.text: The terms “fishery”, “fishery conservation zone”, “fishing”, “fishing vessel”, “Secretary”, and “vessel of the United States” shall each have the same respective meaning as is given to such terms in section 3 of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act ().16 U.S.C. 18021See References in Text note below.1

The term “fishing gear” means any equipment or appurtenance which is necessary for the carrying out of fishing operations by a fishing vessel, whether or not such equipment or appurtenance is attached to such vessel.
The term “fund” means the Fishing Vessel and Gear Damage Compensation Fund established under subsection (f).

The term “resulting economic loss” means the gross income, as estimated by the Secretary, that a fishing vessel owner or operator who is eligible for compensation under this section for damage to, loss of, or destruction of, a fishing vessel or the fishing gear used with such vessel will lose by reason of not being able to engage in fishing, or having to reduce his fishing effort, during the period before the vessel or gear, or both, are repaired or replaced and available for use.

children.heading: Causes of loss or destruction
children.label: b Causes of loss or destruction
A
children.text: occurs when such vessel is engaged in any fishery subject to the exclusive fishery management authority of the United States under the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act [ et seq.], and16 U.S.C. 1801

children.label: B
children.text: is attributable to any vessel (or its crew or fishing gear) other than a vessel of the United States; or

children.label: A
children.text: occurs when such fishing gear is being used for fishing in any fishery subject to such exclusive management authority, and

children.label: B
children.text: is attributable to any other vessel, whether or not such vessel is a vessel of the United States.
makes application to the Secretary for compensation under this section within 90 days after the day on which the damage, loss, or destruction occurred or was first noticed by the owner;
pays upon making such application a reasonable administrative fee which the Secretary shall deposit into the fund;
has, in such form as the Secretary shall prescribe by regulation, a current inventory or other evidence of possession of the fishery vessel or fishing gear concerned;
has complied with all applicable regulations, if any, relating to the marking of, and (if appropriate) the notification of the location of, the fishing gear concerned; and
is in compliance with such other regulations as may be prescribed by the Secretary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Application for compensation; initial determination of eligibility; amount of compensation; review of initial determination; subrogation of United States upon payment
children.label: d Application for compensation; initial determination of eligibility; amount of compensation; review of initial determination; subrogation of United States upon payment

children.text: Application for compensation under this section shall be made in such form and manner, and include such documentation and other evidence relating to the cause and extent of the damage, loss, or destruction, and resulting economic loss, claimed, as the Secretary shall prescribe by regulation. The Secretary shall promptly, but not later than sixty days after receipt of an application, consider, and issue an initial determination with respect to, the application.

children.label: A
children.text: the depreciated replacement cost, or the repair cost, whichever cost is less, of the fishing vessel or the fishing gear concerned; and

children.label: B
children.text: 25 percent of any resulting economic loss.

children.label: A
children.text: if the application is disapproved, set forth the reasons therefor; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the application is approved, set forth the amount of compensation to which the applicant is entitled and the basis on which such amount was determined.

Any vessel owner who is aggrieved by any decision of the Secretary contained in the initial determination of the Secretary regarding such owner’s application may, within thirty days after the date of issue of the initial determination, petition the Secretary for a review of the decision. If petition for review is not made to the Secretary within such thirty-day period regarding the initial determination, the initial determination shall be deemed to be the final determination on the application. Before undertaking any such review, the Secretary shall provide to the vessel owner opportunity to submit additional written or oral evidence relating to the decision. After review the Secretary shall issue a final determination with respect to the application.

If compensation is awarded under the final determination on any application, the Secretary shall promptly pay from the fund to such owner the amount of compensation stated in the final determination. Upon the acceptance of such payment by the vessel owner, the United States shall be subrogated to all rights of the vessel owner with respect to which the payment is made.

children.heading: Surcharge on foreign fishing vessels
children.label: e Surcharge on foreign fishing vessels
children.text: In addition to any fee imposed under section 204(b)(10) of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act () with respect to any foreign fishing vessel for any year after 1978, the Secretary shall impose a surcharge in an amount not to exceed 20 percent of the amount of the fee imposed under such section for such year. The failure to pay any surcharge imposed under this subsection with respect to any foreign fishing vessel shall be treated by the Secretary as a failure to pay the fee for such vessel under such section 204(b)(10).16 U.S.C. 1824(b)(10)

children.heading: Fishing Vessel and Gear Damage Compensation Fund; requirements, etc.
children.label: f Fishing Vessel and Gear Damage Compensation Fund; requirements, etc.

children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States the Fishing Vessel and Gear Damage Compensation Fund. The fund shall be available without fiscal year limitation as a revolving fund for the purposes of administering, and paying compensation awarded under, this section.

children.label: A
children.text: all sums recovered by the United States in the exercise of rights subrogated to it under subsection (d)(5);

children.label: B
children.text: all administrative fees collected under subsection (c)(2);

children.label: C
children.text: all surcharges collected under subsection (e);

children.label: D
children.text: revenues received from deposits or investments made under the last sentence of this paragraph; and

children.label: E
children.text: any revenue acquired through the issuance of obligations under paragraph (3).

Whenever the amount in the fund is not sufficient to pay compensation under this section, the Secretary may issue, in an amount not to exceed $5,000,000, notes or other obligations to the Secretary of the Treasury, in such forms and denominations, bearing such maturities, and subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe. Such notices or other obligations shall bear interest at a rate to be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury on the basis of the current average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States of comparable maturities during the month preceding the issuance of such notices or other obligations. Moneys obtained by the Secretary under this paragraph shall be deposited in the fund and redemptions of any such notices or other obligations shall be made from the fund. The Secretary of the Treasury shall purchase any such notes or other obligations, and for such purpose he may use as a public debt transaction the proceeds from the sale of any securities issued under chapter 31 of title 31. The Secretary of the Treasury may sell any such notices or other obligations at such times and prices and upon such terms and conditions as he shall determine. All purchases, redemptions, and sales of such notes or other obligations by the Secretary of the Treasury shall be treated as public debt transactions of the United States. All borrowing authority contained herein shall be effective only to such extent or in such amounts as are provided in advance in appropriation Acts.2222So in original. Probably should be “notes”.2

children.heading: Penalty for false or misleading statements
children.label: g Penalty for false or misleading statements
children.text: Any person who willfully makes any false or misleading statement or representation for the purpose of obtaining compensation under this section is guilty of a criminal offense and, upon conviction thereof, shall be punished by a fine of not more than $25,000, or by imprisonment for not more than one year, or both.
